🌅 Market News Digest
[Jul 22 4:30 PM – Jul 23 7:00 AM EST]

🔥 Top Stories
• Middle East conflict escalates — fresh U.S. strikes on Iran, tanker attacks in the Red Sea/Hormuz, and oil shipping disruptions drove crude higher and lifted safe-haven bids
• Alphabet (GOOGL) doubles down on AI — FY capex guided to $195B-$205B, Gemini demand remains strong, but investors are weighing heavy cash burn and EU fine risk
• Tesla misses, but keeps spending — weak Q2 profit/free cash flow and ongoing margin pressure as Musk keeps pushing massive AI/robotaxi/battery capex

⛽ Oil & Energy
• Oil spikes on supply fears — WTI topped $90 and Brent neared $96/$100 as attacks hit tankers and U.S./Iran strikes intensified
• Red Sea/Hormuz shipping hit — Saudi- and Chinese-linked tankers targeted; transit risk rising for global crude and LNG flows
• EU Russia sanctions fine-tuned — oil price cap frozen, LNG transfer exemption extended, but more banks/shadow-fleet targets added

📊 Markets & Macro
• Asia stocks mostly higher — gains led by chipmakers; KOSPI surged, Nikkei/Australia also firm despite geopolitics
• Rates and FX reprice — JGB yields hit multi-decade highs, German 10Y reached 3.20%, yen remains weak, euro firmer
• Australia jobs beat hard — June employment jumped 76.3K, boosting AUD and RBA hike bets

🌍 Geopolitical
• Iran conflict widens — reports of strikes near Ahwaz, Bushehr, Jask, and Shalamcheh; CENTCOM says 12th straight night of attacks
• U.S. reinforces region — B-1 bomber deployment and more strikes signal no near-term de-escalation
• Gulf security concerns spread — Kuwait/Bahrain alerts, Oman pushing diplomacy, and Pakistan warning on Houthi threats

🪙 Crypto & DeFi
• BitMEX shuts down — exchange to end all operations Sept. 23
• DeFi hacks continue — Arbitrum/bridge exploits and other protocol breaches keep security in focus
• Tokenized assets grow — Solana tokenized equity value tops $500M as institutional interest broadens

🏛️ Regulatory & Policy
• Google hit with €890M EU fine — DMA breach penalties add regulatory overhang to Alphabet
• U.S. crypto bill advances slowly — Senate Democrats oppose the draft but say talks continue
• Congress insider-trading ban passes House — broad bipartisan messaging on ethics reform

🏢 Corporate
• Big-cap earnings season mixed — RTX, Lockheed, Thermo Fisher, Comcast and T-Mobile beat; Honeywell, Nestlé, STMicro, Tesla and Hyundai disappointed
• Defense names strong — Lockheed backlog jumped 38% and RTX raised outlook, supported by geopolitical demand
• Semis/AI remain active — Intel seeks Ohio fab partner, Samsung/Hynix and Chinese chip access probes add sector noise
• EU autos and industrials — Volkswagen/Japan-style rate moves, European car sales up, and several broker target cuts/raises across GOOGL, IBM, GE Vernova, and others
